title: Dynamical universality in a driven quantum fluid of light
authors: Ivan Gnusov, Paolo Comaron, Antonio Gianfrate, Dimitrios Trypogeorgos, Marzena Szymanska, Paolo Cazzato, Milena De Giorgi, Daniele Sanvitto, Dario Ballarini
abstract: Universal scaling near phase transitions is one of the central ideas of physics, linking the growth of spatial correlations to the slowing down of dynamics. So far, direct experimental access to this critical behavior has remained largely confined to equilibrium many-body systems, and especially to static critical behavior. Here we probe how universality emerges in a driven quantum fluid of light formed by exciton--polaritons in a semiconductor microcavity. By probing the fluctuation-dominated disordered phase below the condensation threshold, we directly measure both the static growth of the correlation length $ξ$ and the dynamical slowing down of the relaxation time $τ$. We find that these quantities obey the universal relation $τ\propto ξ^{z}$ with dynamical exponent $z \approx 2$, revealing diffusive dynamics of a non-conserved order parameter. Our results extend the physics of critical dynamics from equilibrium matter to driven optical systems, bridging quantum condensates and lasers.
summary: This paper demonstrates that a driven-dissipative fluid of light exhibits universal critical dynamics near its condensation threshold. By measuring both spatial and temporal scales, the authors confirm a dynamical exponent of z approximately 2, characterizing the system as a diffusive non-conserved order parameter. This result extends the concept of universality from equilibrium many-body systems to driven optical systems.
main_problem: Determining whether dynamical universality and critical scaling relations, specifically the link between spatial correlations and temporal relaxation, persist in driven-dissipative, non-equilibrium quantum fluids.
main_result: The researchers observed that the system obeys the universal scaling relation tau proportional to xi^z with a dynamical exponent z approximately 2, indicating diffusive dynamics of a non-conserved order parameter.
method: The study combines experimental measurements of spatial coherence and temporal relaxation in polariton condensates with numerical simulations of the stochastic complex Ginzburg-Landau equation.
model_or_system: A driven-dissipative quantum fluid of light formed by exciton-polaritons in a semiconductor microcavity, modeled by a stochastic complex Ginzburg-Landau equation or driven-dissipative Gross-Pitaevskii equation.
key_observables: Correlation length (xi), relaxation time (tau), dynamical exponent (z), static exponent (nu), diffusion coefficient (D), and first-order coherence function (g1).
important_parameters: Reduced pump power (epsilon), excitonic fraction (|X|^2), trap diameter (L), and the control parameter for the phase transition.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the adiabatic approximation where the reservoir follows the condensate, and uses a linearized version of the SCGLE below the threshold.
figures_summary: Figures show the experimental setup, the growth of correlation length and relaxation time near threshold, power-law fits for critical exponents, and data collapse achieved through rescaling for different trap sizes.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of non-equilibrium universality, describes the experimental and numerical platforms, presents the measurement of spatial and temporal scales, demonstrates the scaling relation and universality class, and concludes with a discussion on finite-size effects and robustness.
why_interesting: It provides a rare direct experimental verification of dynamical universality in a non-equilibrium system, bridging the gap between the physics of lasers and equilibrium quantum condensates.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
